title: Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88
content:

{{ShortStory

|title="Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88"

"'''Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88'''" is a short story written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and featured in ''[[Tales of the Bounty Hunters]]'', which was published in [[December]] [[1996]] by [[Bantam Spectra]]. It concerns the backstory of the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88]] as well as its identical counterparts and their efforts to begin a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide droid revolution, starting a few years before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], and ending during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

The title is a reference to [[Wikipedia:Descartes|Descartes's]] philosophical proof of existence, "[[Wikipedia:Cogito ergo sum|''Cogito, ergo sum'']]," or "I think, therefore I am," which features prominently in IG-88's acquisition of sentient artificial intelligence.

==Plot summary==
The [[IG-series/Legends|IG Series Prototype Project]] was the visualization of [[Supervisor/Legends|Imperial Supervisor]] [[Gurdun]] to manufacture the ultimate [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. The [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88]] prototype was activated at the [[Holowan Laboratories/Legends|Holowan Laboratories]]. Due to the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] programming of one of the particular assassin droids, it immediately developed a sense of independence. After dispatching the [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratory]] personnel, [[IG-88A/Legends|IG-88A]] infused his programming into three identical counterparts. Along with the brief help of an older IG unit, [[IG-72]], the four quickly escaped the laboratories, commandeered a [[Starship/Legends|starship]], and made liftoff. Traveling to [[Mechis III/Legends|Mechis III]], they developed a stratagem to seize command over the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].

From [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the four assassins reprogrammed the droids on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] surface, ordering them to depose the [[Human/Legends|human]] caretakers of the droid-manufacturing installation. From here they would begin their quest for galactic domination. To all outward appearances, the administration of Mechis III remained the same, operations continued and orders were filled. The only difference was, the droids being shipped were programmed to efface all biological [[Species/Legends|species]] when the order was given.

After Gurdun issued a "dismantle on sight" order, [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88B]] was sent out to divert attention from the base on Mechis III. Accepting a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] to capture [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], IG-88 found himself on the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. While there, he downloaded the ship's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] files, discovering the construction plans for the [[Death Star II/Legends|new Death Star]]. A new objective presented itself to the foursome: take control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. After planting [[XXt-26 S-thread tracker|microtrackers]] on the ships of the other [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], IG-88B returned to base.

While the assassin droids proceeded with their plans, information was brought to them that another [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], had tracked the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. The ''B'' model was once again sent out to retrieve its bounty. Shortly after arrival in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], IG-88B was destroyed by Fett, who ultimately captured Solo, frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. [[IG-88C/Legends|IG-88C]] and [[IG-88D/Legends|IG-88D]] were dispatched to exact revenge for IG-88B, while the ''A'' version continued the scheme to procure the [[Computer core/Legends|computer core]] of the Death Star II. ''C'' and ''D'' met their demise over the sands of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] at the hands of Boba Fett, and on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] at the [[Mission to Ord Mantell (Dash Rendar)|hands of Dash Rendar]], respectively.

IG-88A did obtain the computer core of the Death Star and assimilated himself into its programming. The realization of their goal was short-lived, however, as the space station was destroyed by the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel fleet]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Continuity==
The story ends with [[IG-88A/Legends|IG-88]]as the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station|second Death Star]], and readers familiar with the outcome of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] ([[1983]]) understand what fate ultimately befalls the would-be conqueror [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. Throughout the story, there are descriptions of IG-88's physical form that are inconsistent with rather the spindly droid seen in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] ([[1980]]). For example, references are made to IG-88 having fingers and bulky armor, and weighing “several metric tons.”<ref name="TERC" />
